message:
feat(frankenpandas): add read_csv_with_options_path to prelude (fd90.215 / br-4ns5p)

Completes the [path/string × no-options/with-options] CSV reader
matrix in the prelude:

  | input  | no-options          | with-options                    |
  |--------|---------------------|---------------------------------|
  | path   | read_csv ✓          | read_csv_with_options_path NEW  |
  | string | read_csv_str ✓      | read_csv_with_options ✓         |

The path+options variant was the only quadrant missing from the
prelude. Same half-surface pattern as fd90.207/209/210/211: the type
(CsvReadOptions) and three other reader functions were reachable, but
the symmetric path-based reader was not.

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"frankenpandas-4m90b","title":"fd90.183: README Quick Start + case_when use new Scalar From .into() ergonomics","description":"fd90.74-182 follow-on. fd90.182 added From<bool/i64/f64/&str/String> impls for Scalar. Update the README's Quick Start example to use the new ergonomic .into() pattern so readers see the cleaner syntax instead of the explicit Scalar::Utf8(...) form.

Before (line 222):
  let dates = Series::from_values("d", vec![0i64.into()], vec![Scalar::Utf8("2024-01-15".into())])?;

After (cleaner via fd90.182):
  let dates = Series::from_values("d", vec![0i64.into()], vec!["2024-01-15".into()])?;

Also updates the case_when example (lines 1086-1090) where Vec<Scalar> values are constructed via repeat for "Good"/"Poor"/"Excellent" — the inner Scalar::Utf8(...) wrappers can become "Good".into() etc.

Pure README change — fd90.182 already shipped the From impls.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:08:36.244959583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:12:33.417764029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:12:33.415681515Z","close_reason":"Updated README Quick Start (line 222) and case_when example (lines 1086-1090) to use the new From<&str>/From<i64>/etc ergonomics introduced by fd90.182. Mirrored the same change in tests/readme_quick_example.rs:60. All 22 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-4ns5p","title":"fd90.215: Add read_csv_with_options_path to prelude","description":"fd90.215: Add read_csv_with_options_path to the prelude.

Completes the CSV reader matrix in the prelude:
- read_csv (path, no options) — IN prelude
- read_csv_str (string, no options) — IN prelude
- read_csv_with_options (string + CsvReadOptions) — IN prelude (fd90.207)
- read_csv_with_options_path (path + CsvReadOptions) — NOT in prelude

Same half-surface pattern as fd90.207/209/210/211. With read_csv_with_options in the prelude, users have a CsvReadOptions in scope; the natural symmetric expectation is that both string-input and path-input variants are reachable.

Scope:
- Add read_csv_with_options_path to the prelude
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:34:43.336696546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:36:26.272647187Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:36:26.270659402Z","close_reason":"Added read_csv_with_options_path to the prelude — completes the [path/string × no-options/with-options] CSV reader matrix. Other 3 quadrants (read_csv path, read_csv_str string, read_csv_with_options string+opts) already in prelude. Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard.  {"id":"frankenpandas-4r56","title":"[MEDIUM] parity: Timedelta arithmetic + TZ-aware datetime ops","description":"Parity-gap audit: datetime + Timedelta arithmetic, TZ-aware operations.

**Scope.** pandas:
- Timestamp + Timedelta → Timestamp.
- Timestamp - Timestamp → Timedelta.
- Timedelta + Timedelta → Timedelta.
- Timedelta * scalar → Timedelta.
- tz-aware: `pd.Timestamp('2024-01-01', tz='US/Eastern') + pd.Timedelta(days=1)` stays in the same tz.
- DST transitions: adding 1 day across a spring-forward boundary changes wall-clock hour but preserves absolute duration.

**FrankenPandas state.** fp-types has `Timedelta` namespace with NANOS_PER_* constants + parse(). No Timestamp type surfaced publicly; datetime values live as Utf8-encoded ISO 8601 strings per README Limitations.

Gaps (from README + existing DISCREPANCIES):
- No native Timestamp dtype → TZ-aware arithmetic not possible.
- Timedelta arithmetic: `Timedelta + Timedelta` not exposed as a pub fn; only internals.
- Scalar::Timedelta64 exists but no `Scalar::Timestamp` / `Scalar::DatetimeTz`.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — datetime handling is pandas's foundational surface; partial coverage limits financial/time-series workflows. chrono-tz is already a workspace dep per Cargo.toml inspection, so IANA tz data is available.

**Fix shape.** Phased:
- Phase 1: expose Timedelta + Timedelta arithmetic as pub fns in fp-types.
- Phase 2: add Timestamp struct (i64 nanos since Unix epoch, optional chrono_tz::Tz).
- Phase 3: live-oracle fixtures for dt arithmetic: Timestamp + Timedelta, Timestamp - Timestamp, tz-aware shift across DST, leap-second handling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T02:12:19.958740286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T02:20:08.283613382Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T02:20:08.281014037Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datetime","parity-gap","review","timedelta","tz"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4r5t5","title":"fd90.124: README error table shows LengthMismatch as unit variant but actual is struct in both FrameError and IndexError","description":"fd90.74-123 README audit — Error Architecture table at lines 821-830. Two rows list 'LengthMismatch' as if it were a unit variant, alongside other struct-variant entries that correctly use braces:

  | IndexError | ... | OutOfBounds { position, length }, LengthMismatch, ... |
  | FrameError | ... | LengthMismatch, DuplicateIndexUnsupported, ... |

But both are struct variants:
- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:111 — FrameError::LengthMismatch { index_len, column_len }
- crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs:1344 — IndexError::LengthMismatch { expected, actual, context }

The README has been progressively normalized (see fd90.101 for MissingIndexColumn). 'LengthMismatch' is the last remaining unit-typed-looking entry that's actually a struct variant.

Also: TypeError row shows IncompatibleDtypes { left, right } correctly with braces, so the README convention is established — the LengthMismatch entries just weren't updated.

Fix:
- IndexError row: LengthMismatch → LengthMismatch { expected, actual, context }
- FrameError row: LengthMismatch → LengthMismatch { index_len, column_len }

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:38:58.855538828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:40:13.784292249Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:40:13.781684259Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-4vno","title":"fp-io SQL query builder helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: extract and test backend-neutral SQL query builder helpers for SELECT projections and write-side CREATE/INSERT statement construction. Keep this separate from pane 3's Postgres/schema adapter work; no new files. Reuse existing identifier validation/escaping and parameter_marker/type mapping hooks, add focused fp-io tests for table SELECT, projected SELECT, CREATE table with named index, backend-specific insert markers, and invalid identifiers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cod-pandas-4","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:54.714604899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:32:08.639397670Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:32:08.637399347Z","close_reason":"Scope completed in parallel via fd90.x sub-beads on cc-pandas-2: backend-neutral SQL query builder helpers ship as sql_select_all_query_in_schema (fd90.14), sql_select_columns_query_in_schema (fd90.34), sql_create_table_query_in_schema (fd90.16), sql_insert_rows_query_in_schema (fd90.15), sql_multi_row_insert_query_in_schema (fd90.19), sql_drop_table_query_in_schema (fd90.16). All route through identifier validation + conn.quote_identifier + conn.parameter_marker. Tests cover bare-table SELECT, projected SELECT, schema-qualified CREATE/INSERT, backend marker overrides, and invalid identifier rejection (sql_select_all_query_*, sql_create_table_query_*, sql_insert_rows_query_*, write_sql_* tests in fp-io). cod-pandas-4 was stalled since 2026-04-25 (cod agents at usage limits until 2026-04-30); reassigning to cc-pandas-2 retroactively to reflect actual completion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
